title: matthiola odoratissima subsp. tatarica
intro: Matthiola odoratissima subsp. tatarica, also known as Tatar stock or Tatar stock, is a flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its clusters of pink to purple fragrant flowers and its tall, upright stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Matthiola odoratissima subsp. tatarica include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
